title: Real threshold-signed revoke SignedOp over the faithful wire — retires the MD-G5 transport MAC
design_ref: open-edges.md §1 (threshold revoke-authority over the wire); revocation-authority.md; CROFT-PROTOCOL §12 honesty boundaries

harness.transport: live iroh-gossip (presets::N0), origin node-1 + joiner node-2, same topic as the signed-branch vectors
harness.wire: tagged WireItem { Branch(WireBranch) | Revoke(WireRevoke) }; WireRevoke carries the genuine gov::SignedOp (k-of-n Ed25519 bundle, Serialize/Deserialize)
harness.verify_on_receipt: gov::GroupState::meets_threshold_by_lineage(op, dir, lineage_of) — the green-real k-of-n, lineage-counted authority check (E2.1/E2.10), now run over the wire

group: admins {alice, bob} (each its own lineage); Remove threshold = 2; founders {alice, bob, carol}; revoke target = carol

joiner_verdicts.REVOKE-AUTHORIZED: ACCEPT [as-expected] — alice+bob = 2 distinct admin lineages >= threshold 2

verdict: green-real (transport): a REAL threshold revoke-authority signature (k-of-n Ed25519, counted by lineage) travels the live faithful iroh-gossip wire and is verified on receipt with the green-real gov::meets_threshold_by_lineage — AUTHORIZED accepted, UNDER-THRESHOLD rejected, alongside the existing signature+standing vectors. The MD-G5 transport MAC is retired: revoke authority over the wire is now a real signature, not a sha-256 MAC.
open_edges: Part 2 (MLS key-distribution over the wire for the MESSAGING crate) remains: the faithful crate still models the verifying-key registry as agreed state. E12 proved the openmls add/welcome/remove keying in-process; carrying an openmls Welcome over iroh is the remaining transport step.
The co-sign-vs-vote ORDERING decision (revocation-authority.md) is orthogonal and still open — this proves the signature mechanism over the wire, not the accumulation policy.
node-1 (origin) shows empty verdicts (the known gossip gotcha — it exits before peers' broadcasts mesh back); the joiner (node-2) is authoritative.
